value.body:
Found during a follow-up static issue scan of the Cycle codebase.

Problem:
Bearer token comparison can authorize an invalid token if `Crypto.digest` fails. The error path replaces both digests with empty byte arrays, and `constantTimeEqual` returns true for two empty arrays.

Evidence:
- `packages/api/src/http/handlers/crypto.ts:31-37` digests the supplied and expected tokens, but catches any digest failure and returns `[new Uint8Array(), new Uint8Array()]`.
- `packages/api/src/http/handlers/crypto.ts:42-50` returns true when both arrays are length 0 and no byte differences exist.
- `packages/api/src/http/handlers/Authorization.ts:21-30` uses that boolean directly to authorize or reject the bearer credential.

Impact:
A platform crypto failure should fail closed. Today it can fail open and accept any supplied bearer token under that failure mode.

Suggested remediation:
- On digest failure, return `false` or fail the effect and map that failure to unauthorized.
- Add a targeted unit test where `Crypto.digest` fails for supplied/expected values and assert authorization is denied.
- Consider removing catch-all swallowing in token comparison so future crypto failures are observable.

Acceptance criteria:
- `timingSafeTokenEqualWithCrypto` cannot return true when digesting fails.
- Authorization returns 401 for digest failures.
- Regression tests cover the fail-closed behavior.

frontmatter.title: Fix API bearer auth fail-open path when crypto digest fails

value.title: Fix API bearer auth fail-open path when crypto digest fails
